title: 7 Reasons Teens Say No to AI
title_cn: 青少年对人工智能说“不”的7个理由

paragraphs.cn: 2026年1月31日 美国东部时间上午8:00

paragraphs.en: While adults worry about sharpening their artificial-intelligence skills to remain relevant in their jobs, many of their teenage children are feeling meh about the AI revolution.
paragraphs.cn: 当成年人担心提高他们的人工智能技能以在工作中保持竞争力时，他们的许多青少年孩子却对人工智能革命感到不屑一顾。

paragraphs.en: I wondered if it was just my teenagers, whose eyes glaze over every time my husband and I urge them to learn more about AI. Parents can’t help but worry about how a lack of AI preparedness will affect their kids’ future career prospects.
paragraphs.cn: 我曾想过这是否只是我的青少年孩子们的想法，每当我丈夫和我敦促他们多了解人工智能时，他们的眼神就会变得呆滞。父母们不禁担心缺乏人工智能准备会如何影响他们孩子的未来职业前景。

paragraphs.en: I reached out to dozens of teens and found many who share my kids’ reluctance to embrace AI—as well as their fears about where it’s headed.
paragraphs.cn: 我联系了几十名青少年，发现许多人与我的孩子一样不愿接受人工智能——以及他们对人工智能发展方向的担忧。

paragraphs.en: “I think it’s bad for creativity,” said Willow Nolan, a 16-year-old in Los Angeles. “You just put the prompt in and it makes whatever you want. When you use it excessively, it’s hard to have ideas of your own.”
paragraphs.cn: “我认为这对创造力有害，”16岁的洛杉矶女孩柳·诺兰说。“你只需输入提示，它就会生成你想要的任何东西。如果你过度使用它，就很难有自己的想法。”

paragraphs.en: It isn’t that teens don’t use chatbots—about two-thirds do, according to a recent report from Pew Research Center. But the teens I spoke to say they have curtailed their use because of fears over cheating accusations, concerns about AI’s environmental impact and worries about negative psychological effects.
paragraphs.cn: 这并不是说青少年不使用聊天机器人——根据皮尤研究中心最近的一份报告，大约三分之二的青少年使用。但我采访的青少年表示，他们已经减少了使用，因为担心作弊指控、对人工智能环境影响的担忧以及对负面心理影响的担忧。

paragraphs.en: “I will use AI sometimes, but it’s a last resort for me,” said Leo Gurney, a 19-year-old college student in London.
paragraphs.cn: “我有时会使用人工智能，但对我来说，那是最后的选择，”19岁的伦敦大学生利奥·格尼说。

paragraphs.en: Whether this represents a small group of holdouts or a coming generational shift remains to be seen. We know generative AI is capable of bringing positive changes in the form of scientific discovery, office-productivity improvements, support for people who are lonely and much more.
paragraphs.cn: 这代表着一小部分坚持者还是即将到来的代际转变，仍有待观察。我们知道生成式人工智能能够带来积极的变化，例如科学发现、办公效率提升、为孤独者提供支持等等。

paragraphs.en: Yet these teens, who have grown up witnessing the problems wrought by social media, are taking a cautious approach. These are their biggest concerns.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，这些在目睹社交媒体所带来的问题中长大的青少年，正在采取一种谨慎的态度。这些是他们最大的担忧。

paragraphs.en: “It freaks me out that people are choosing to talk to fake people instead of socializing, and I worry it could become an addiction,” said Annika Frederick, a 14-year-old in Denver who only occasionally uses ChatGPT—and always in a web browser. “I don’t want to commit to downloading it on my phone.”
paragraphs.cn: “人们选择与虚拟人物交谈而不是社交，这让我感到不安，我担心这可能会成为一种瘾，”14岁的丹佛女孩安妮卡·弗雷德里克说，她只偶尔使用ChatGPT——而且总是在网页浏览器中使用。“我不想承诺在手机上下载它。”

paragraphs.en: Many teens (and adults) turn to AI for advice or companionship. A recent Common Sense Media survey found that about one in three teens have used chatbots for friendship, romantic interactions or emotional support.
paragraphs.cn: 许多青少年（和成年人）转向人工智能寻求建议或陪伴。Common Sense Media最近的一项调查发现，大约三分之一的青少年曾使用聊天机器人来寻求友谊、浪漫互动或情感支持。

paragraphs.en: “We don’t know the lasting effects of talking to AI. It might make it hard to talk to people,” Willow said.
paragraphs.cn: “我们不知道与人工智能交谈的持久影响。它可能会让人难以与人交谈，”柳说。

paragraphs.en: “Putting in a prompt and saying ‘Draw me this’ doesn’t feel right to me,” said Leo, who is studying esports management. “It takes the joy out of listening to a song if I learn it was made by AI, no matter how good it sounds.”
paragraphs.cn: “输入提示说‘给我画这个’，对我来说感觉不对，”正在学习电子竞技管理的利奥说。“如果我得知一首歌是人工智能创作的，无论它听起来多好，都会让我失去听歌的乐趣。”

paragraphs.en: “Chatbots are stealing artists’ work and I have a moral objection to that,” Annika said.
paragraphs.cn: “聊天机器人正在窃取艺术家的作品，我对此有道德上的反对，”安妮卡说。

paragraphs.en: “It requires so much space to make all those data centers that house all the data that is used by AI,” said Ruby Mehta, a 12-year-old in suburban Washington, D.C. “It is taking up a lot of resources, which is contributing to climate change and habitat loss and a bunch of other things.”
paragraphs.cn: “建造所有容纳人工智能所需数据的数据中心需要占用大量空间，”12岁的华盛顿郊区女孩鲁比·梅塔说。“它正在消耗大量资源，这导致了气候变化、栖息地丧失以及许多其他问题。”

paragraphs.en: Ohannes Montgomery, a 15-year-old in Simi Valley, Calif., said he once used ChatGPT every couple of days until three months ago, when he quit, in part, after learning how much water is used to cool the data centers.
paragraphs.cn: 15岁的加利福尼亚州西米谷男孩奥汉内斯·蒙哥马利说，他曾经每隔几天就使用ChatGPT，直到三个月前他放弃了，部分原因是了解到冷却数据中心需要消耗大量水。

paragraphs.en: “I feel like it will make people lazy. If they’re stuck on something they’ll always go to AI,” said Chase Morgan, a 13-year-old in Grapevine, Texas.
paragraphs.cn: “我觉得它会让人变懒。如果他们遇到困难，他们总是会求助于人工智能，”13岁的得克萨斯州格雷普韦恩男孩蔡斯·摩根说。

paragraphs.en: He said when he needs tips on fixing his dirt bike, he prefers to search for things on the internet or to use YouTube. His twin brother, Pierce, however, uses AI daily. He said understanding it is a prerequisite for success.
paragraphs.cn: 他说，当他需要修理越野摩托车的技巧时，他更喜欢在互联网上搜索或使用YouTube。然而，他的双胞胎兄弟皮尔斯每天都使用人工智能。他说，理解人工智能是成功的先决条件。

paragraphs.en: “If you use AI for everything, you could start being dependent on it,” Ohannes said.
paragraphs.cn: “如果你什么都用人工智能，你可能会开始依赖它，”奥汉内斯说。

paragraphs.en: Hadar Groner, an 18-year-old high-school senior in Yonkers, N.Y., said she used AI sparingly. “When I took chemistry, I would ask AI for help balancing equations and it couldn’t do that. It was giving me things that didn’t make sense. That was a couple of years ago, so it might have gotten better, but I always try googling things first before turning to AI.”
paragraphs.cn: 18岁的纽约扬克斯高中生哈达尔·格罗纳说，她很少使用人工智能。“当我学化学时，我曾向人工智能寻求帮助平衡方程式，但它做不到。它给我的东西毫无意义。那是几年前的事了，所以现在可能已经改进了，但我总是先尝试谷歌搜索，然后再求助于人工智能。”

paragraphs.en: Ruby has also gotten wrong answers, including one related to her pet snake. “I asked AI, ‘What do you get a snake for their birthday?’ and it said a trip to the zoo.”
paragraphs.cn: 鲁比也曾得到过错误的答案，包括一个关于她的宠物蛇的问题。“我问人工智能，‘蛇过生日送什么？’它说‘去动物园旅行’。”

paragraphs.en: “I’m afraid of it storing data about me,” Hadar said. “I guess that happens with any online thing, but something about sharing personal data with AI feels sketchy. I don’t fully understand what they’re doing with that info.”
paragraphs.cn: “我担心它会存储我的数据，”哈达尔说。“我想任何在线事物都会这样，但与人工智能分享个人数据总觉得有点可疑。我不太完全理解他们会如何处理这些信息。”

paragraphs.en: Leo said he was troubled by how AI has been used to create fake nude images of people and how it has fed peoples’ delusions. He read a recent story I wrote about a murder-suicide that was preceded by the killer’s heavy ChatGPT use. “It gave me the shivers,” he said.
paragraphs.cn: 利奥说，他对人工智能被用来创建人们的假裸照以及它如何助长人们的妄想感到不安。他读了我最近写的一篇关于一起谋杀-自杀事件的报道，该事件发生前，凶手曾大量使用ChatGPT。“这让我不寒而栗，”他说。

paragraphs.en: “If we rely on AI too much,” Leo added, “we may lose our humanity.”
paragraphs.cn: “如果我们过于依赖人工智能，”利奥补充道，“我们可能会失去我们的人性。”

paragraphs.en: Write to Julie Jargon at Julie.Jargon@wsj.com
paragraphs.cn: 请发送邮件至 Julie Jargon (Julie.Jargon@wsj.com)

vocabulary.meaning_en: Closely connected or appropriate to what is being done or considered.
vocabulary.example: While adults worry about sharpening their artificial-intelligence skills to remain relevant in their jobs, many of their teenage children are feeling meh about the AI revolution.
vocabulary.example_cn: 当成年人担心提高他们的人工智能技能以在工作中保持竞争力时，他们的许多青少年孩子却对人工智能革命感到不屑一顾。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Unwillingness or disinclination to do something.
vocabulary.example: I reached out to dozens of teens and found many who share my kids’ reluctance to embrace AI—as well as their fears about where it’s headed.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我联系了几十名青少年，发现许多人与我的孩子一样不愿接受人工智能——以及他们对人工智能发展方向的担忧。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Reduced in extent or quantity; imposed a restriction on.
vocabulary.example: But the teens I spoke to say they have curtailed their use because of fears over cheating accusations, concerns about AI’s environmental impact and worries about negative psychological effects.
vocabulary.example_cn: 但我采访的青少年表示，他们已经减少了使用，因为担心作弊指控、对人工智能环境影响的担忧以及对负面心理影响的担忧。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A charge or claim that someone has done something illegal or wrong.
vocabulary.example: But the teens I spoke to say they have curtailed their use because of fears over cheating accusations, concerns about AI’s environmental impact and worries about negative psychological effects.
vocabulary.example_cn: 但我采访的青少年表示，他们已经减少了使用，因为担心作弊指控、对人工智能环境影响的担忧以及对负面心理影响的担忧。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Of, affecting, or arising in the mind; related to the mental and emotional state of a person.
vocabulary.example: But the teens I spoke to say they have curtailed their use because of fears over cheating accusations, concerns about AI’s environmental impact and worries about negative psychological effects.
vocabulary.example_cn: 但我采访的青少年表示，他们已经减少了使用，因为担心作弊指控、对人工智能环境影响的担忧以及对负面心理影响的担忧。

vocabulary.meaning_en: Relating to or capable of production or reproduction.
vocabulary.example: We know generative AI is capable of bringing positive changes in the form of scientific discovery, office-productivity improvements, support for people who are lonely and much more.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们知道生成式人工智能能够带来积极的变化，例如科学发现、办公效率提升、为孤独者提供支持等等。

vocabulary.meaning_en: (archaic or literary) fashioned or created; (of metals) beaten out or shaped by hammering.
vocabulary.meaning_cn: 造成；带来 (通常指不好的结果)
vocabulary.example: Yet these teens, who have grown up witnessing the problems wrought by social media, are taking a cautious approach.
vocabulary.example_cn: 然而，这些在目睹社交媒体所带来的问题中长大的青少年，正在采取一种谨慎的态度。

vocabulary.meaning_en: (of a person) careful to avoid potential problems or dangers.
vocabulary.example: Yet these teens, who have grown up witnessing the problems wrought by social media, are taking a cautious approach.
vocabulary.example_cn: 然而，这些在目睹社交媒体所带来的问题中长大的青少年，正在采取一种谨慎的态度。

vocabulary.meaning_en: The friendly company or society of others; a feeling of fellowship or friendship.
vocabulary.example: Many teens (and adults) turn to AI for advice or companionship.
vocabulary.example_cn: 许多青少年（和成年人）转向人工智能寻求建议或陪伴。

vocabulary.meaning_en: A thing that is required as a prior condition for something else to happen or exist.
vocabulary.example: His twin brother, Pierce, however, uses AI daily. He said understanding it is a prerequisite for success.
vocabulary.example_cn: 然而，他的双胞胎兄弟皮尔斯每天都使用人工智能。他说，理解人工智能是成功的先决条件。
